selected_plan.title: Add Korean localization to WhatIfInvest
selected_plan.summary: Use system-language-driven localization resources plus a small central string layer so the current WhatIfInvest experience reads naturally in English and Korean.

retained_alternatives.title: Add an in-app language switcher
retained_alternatives.summary: Expose an app preference for English and Korean inside the product UI.
pros: Avoids dependence on system language settings
cons: Widens scope into settings UX and preference persistence
Not required by the current request

retained_alternatives.title: Inline locale checks per view
retained_alternatives.summary: Translate strings by branching on locale directly inside each SwiftUI view.
pros: Fast for one or two strings
cons: Easy to miss dynamic strings and trust copy

nodes.title: Ship a natural Korean experience for WhatIfInvest
nodes.description: Make the current WhatIfInvest product usable in Korean without weakening the existing English-first flow.
nodes.why: The app needs bilingual reach, and awkward literal translation would hurt trust and perceived polish.
acceptance_criteria: Primary app surfaces appear in Korean on Korean devices
constraints: Do not add a separate settings flow
assumptions: Supporting Korean only is enough for this pass
risks: Dynamic strings may be missed if localization is too view-specific

nodes.title: Add a maintainable localization layer
nodes.description: Back the app with English and Korean string resources plus a small central localizer helper.
nodes.why: A maintainable localization pass needs one durable source of truth for text and formatting.
acceptance_criteria: String resources exist for both locales
constraints: Keep the change compatible with the current Xcode project layout
assumptions: The existing SwiftUI structure can stay intact

nodes.title: Cover the full product flow with natural copy
nodes.description: Localize navigation, scenario editing, result summaries, trust notes, share copy, and validation messages.
nodes.why: Users notice incomplete localization most in dynamic results and trust messaging, not only in buttons.
constraints: Keep product meaning intact in Korean

nodes.title: Add English and Korean localization resources
nodes.description: Create the shared localization tables and launch-screen resources needed for English fallback and Korean UI copy.
nodes.why: The app needs a durable translation source instead of scattered literals.
acceptance_criteria: English and Korean string resources exist for the app surface
The project recognizes Korean as a supported localization
constraints: Keep the existing project file layout intact
assumptions: Base English strings remain the fallback
risks: Project localization regions may not be updated
done_criteria: English and Korean string resources exist for the app surface
The project recognizes Korean as a supported localization

verification.description: Inspect the resource tables and project settings to confirm both locales are wired correctly.

nodes.title: Route UI and dynamic copy through a central localizer
nodes.description: Replace hard-coded English strings across views, formatting helpers, model messages, and error copy with localized accessors and formatters.
nodes.why: A partial localization pass would leave the Korean experience visibly incomplete.
acceptance_criteria: Visible UI labels and trust copy are localized
Dynamic summary and validation strings are localized
constraints: Do not break existing view structure
assumptions: A central helper can cover both static and formatted text cleanly
risks: String reuse can accidentally mix contexts if keys are poorly separated
done_criteria: Visible UI labels and trust copy are localized
Dynamic summary and validation strings are localized

verification.description: Review every user-facing string path in the app to confirm it now resolves through localized resources.

nodes.title: Verify the localized app still builds and preserves English fallback
nodes.description: Build the app and update focused tests where the new localization structure changes behavior or selectors.
nodes.why: Localization changes touch many text surfaces and can easily break identifiers or project resources.
acceptance_criteria: Existing English-path tests still make sense
Core localized surfaces are covered by verification
constraints: Keep English as the default automated test path
assumptions: Focused verification is enough for this pass
risks: UI tests coupled to text can become brittle
done_criteria: Existing English-path tests still make sense
Core localized surfaces are covered by verification

verification.description: Build the WhatIfInvest scheme after localization changes.
Run focused app tests that exercise the localized flow.

risk_summary.description: Some dynamic or less obvious strings could remain in English if the pass is not exhaustive.
risk_summary.mitigation: Centralize formatted strings and re-scan the codebase for user-facing copy.

risk_summary.description: Literal Korean copy could feel machine-translated and reduce trust.
risk_summary.mitigation: Use meaning-first Korean phrasing and keep finance brand names recognizable.

risk_summary.description: UI tests that rely on visible English copy can break once localization is introduced.
risk_summary.mitigation: Prefer accessibility identifiers where possible and keep English fallback intact for the default test path.
